https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/24660/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Why don't we just make a new OBD_CONNECT_DIR_STRIPE flag to avoid such problems in the future?  It is likely that OBD_CONNECT_LFSCK will also be backported to 2.5.x. 

Please use 0x400000000000000 for this, since the earlier flags are already reserved:

  #define OBD_CONNECT_UNLINK_CLOSE 0x100000000000000ULL/* close file in unlink */

And I think I told Jinshan he could use the 0x200... Flag, but I don't have that email handy right now.

Patch Set 5:

This patch has failed sanity four times in the last 8 runs.  LU-4804 twice (create remote dir error), LU-5240 (ZFS), aborted once, and once it just stopped in the middle of the test without any logs or obvious failures.

It might just be coincidence, but this should be checked with the patch is refreshed.

commit.subject: LU-4843 mdt: Add OBD_CONNECT_DIR_STRIPE flag
commit.message:
LU-4843 mdt: Add OBD_CONNECT_DIR_STRIPE flag

Add OBD_CONNECT_DIR_STRIPE to tell if the client supports
striped dir, so only new client (>= 2.6) can access striped
directory.

Change-Id: I5d5f1a6a9f5efdce0c6fe22106a8853cd78bb486
Signed-off-by: Wang Di <di.wang@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10773
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
